evidence: Vrijen described himself as becoming a '1% or 0.1% power user' of products he embeds with—yet he simultaneously identifies as 'non-technical but increasingly trying to push my boundaries.'
Vrijen has moved 12 cities across 33 years—bulk of that in first half of his life... Perhaps frequent relocation doesn't just make you adaptable—it makes you *good at noticing* adaptation.

source_display: Human Support Systems vs Human-Centered AI
evidence: Zain's entire thesis centers on what he calls 'human support systems'—infrastructure needed to navigate an AI-automated future.

source_display: Small World Algorithm as Trust Mechanism
evidence: The Logan connection—Zain randomly connected with Vrijen's co-founder on TikTok, and that morning both mentioned this 'interesting person they were talking to'—creates a 'glitch in the matrix' moment.
Vrijen calling it a 'complete luxury' and 'indulgence' to enjoy setting up your workspace... Maybe all productivity is just identity performance.

source_display: Authenticity as a Moat Against Clickbait
evidence: Zain noted staying off social media for years because of the toxic arguments, but wanting to share ideas... Authentic content creates trust slowly and transactionally.
Vrijen stated there is 'zero way you could pay for my integrity because my belief in a product I like is too strong.'
Vrijen described using AI to 'compensate for those gaps' (bad memory, rashness) and be a 'cognitive amplifier'... The AI doesn't make him smarter; it prevents him from being stupid.

When you reach sufficient density in your professional graph, unorchestrated serendipity becomes statistically probable... Vrijen's strategy of 'being active in multiple spaces' maximizes graph intersection probability.
Zain had randomly connected with Logan (Vrijen's co-founder) on TikTok after seeing her content about work and skills.
Careerspan isn't a replacement for Handshake or any recruiting platform—it's the 'missing layer' that makes their products defensible.

Handshake has 1,600 universities but no way to capture behavioral signal at scale. Careerspan has the coaching infrastructure to capture signal but lacks distribution. The combination creates a data moat neither could build alone.
Positioning Careerspan as a strategic hedge for Handshake's declining core business ($200M, only 6% growth).
Both Vrijen and Logan are seeing surprising social media growth (20% and 2,000 followers respectively) with minimal posting.

The entire Careerspan thesis rests on the premise that behavioral signal (how people think) is more valuable than profile data (what they've done). This challenges the fundamental premise of LinkedIn and traditional resumes.
Emphasizing the 3-person team as an asset (low acquisition overhead) turns a potential weakness into strength... we're not expensive to acquire, but we're strategically valuable.
Logan to send one pager to Jennifer (FindThem), Tech Buffalo, Juice Box, Korn Fairy, Camina one pager, Handshake.
